title: hieracium laevigatum subsp. boreanum
intro: Hieracium laevigatum subsp. boreanum, also known as boreal hawk's-beard, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. The herbal preparations of Hieracium laevigatum subsp. boreanum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or using a solvent like ethanol.
